overview: The Trados-Tagged RTF Filter reads RTF files prepared with the Trados translation layer of styles. It processes documents where HTML/XML tags are visible and marked with Trados styles like `tw4wInternal` (red) and `tw4wExternal` (gray), rather than "normal" RTF files with true RTF formatting codes. **This is a read-only filter** — it cannot generate translated output documents.
limitations: **Read-only filter** — does not generate output corresponding to the input. Must be used for input/extraction only.
Only understands inline codes represented with Trados styles (`tw4wInternal`, `tw4wExternal`), not RTF with "normal" formatting.
Only text within Trados segmentation markers is extracted — any text outside segment markers is ignored and treated as non-translatable.
"Normal" RTF files translated with Trados (showing segments with true RTF formatting) are **not** Trados-Tagged RTF and cannot be processed by this filter.
processingNotes: Encoding is determined from font encoding information declared in the RTF document. If no default encoding is declared, the user-specified encoding is used as fallback.
Only text marked by Trados segmentation markers is extracted. All text outside segment markers is ignored and considered non-translatable.
A valid Trados-Tagged RTF file has HTML/XML tags visible in the text marked with `tw4wInternal` or `tw4wExternal` styles, rather than rendered as actual RTF formatting.
